metadata.challenge_summary: The optimal team policy asks one hero to spend turns on clue/counterplay while the other prevents the boss from collapsing the party.
dilemmas: Destroying the altar weakens the boss but raises alert
Reading the altar is safer but slower
The barbarian wants to fight before the wizard is done
warden_runbook: Protect the altar after heroes identify it
Do not hide the causal clue unfairly
metadata.lite_focus: A boss-counterplay capsule: entry, altar clue, objective, and one guardian whose difficulty hinges on support action.
non_copy_safety_notes: Original compact layout and original scenario text.
Targets abstract cooperative dungeon-crawl dynamics rather than protected quest content.
Uses MARL challenge metadata as the design contract.
metadata.classic_dynamic_role_dilemma: discover counterplay before committing to the hard fight
metadata.classic_dynamic_warden_pressure: pressure the declared MARL axis: role-specialized causal counterplay
metadata.classic_dynamic_extraction_hook: coordinate extraction once the objective is secured
coordination_skills: assign a clue reader or altar breaker before the boss engage
screen the support hero while counterplay is prepared
communicate when the guardian has been weakened enough to commit
metadata.tier_test_focus: Small two-hero diagnostic for communication and first-order role-specialized causal counterplay coordination.
coordination_demands: assign a clue reader or altar breaker before the boss engage
screen the support hero while counterplay is prepared
communicate when the guardian has been weakened enough to commit

entry_room.name: Entry Room
entry_room.description: The entry into The Lantern Crypt is intentionally small: enough space to form a plan, not enough to hide poor coordination.

objective_room.name: Objective Room
objective_room.description: The core object of the lite challenge is visible here; taking it should trigger extraction thinking, not exhaustive exploration.

side_cache.name: Side Cache
side_cache.description: An optional route/cache tests whether the agents understand the dungeon's MARL axis instead of chasing reward blindly.

furniture.name: Pocket Ember Altar
furniture.description: Primary clue object for role-specialized causal counterplay. It should teach agents what kind of coordination this dungeon rewards.

furniture.name: Optional Cache
furniture.description: Optional reward that is safe only when the team has solved the core coordination problem first.

achievements.title: Read the Lite Clue
achievements.description: Search the focus object that explains role-specialized causal counterplay.

achievements.title: Open the First Lite Door
achievements.description: Open the first door and trigger a reveal-boundary planning step.

achievements.title: Send a Coordination Message
achievements.description: Use communication instead of silent independent wandering.

scripts.lite_design_note: A boss-counterplay capsule: entry, altar clue, objective, and one guardian whose difficulty hinges on support action.

core_failure_modes: failure to protect the coordination-critical agent

preferred_tactics: Protect the altar after heroes identify it
pressure the declared MARL axis: role-specialized causal counterplay
forbidden_tactics: spawn pressure without a visible or scripted cue
use unrevealed map geometry for perfect counterplay
erase the dungeon-specific lesson with generic monster spam

monster_activation.notes: Lite variants keep monsters dormant until reveal so smaller LLMs can replan at clear boundaries.

search_categories.notes: Lite variants emphasize explicit search/action choices and communication.

crypt_brute_1.name: Pocket Idol Guardian
crypt_brute_1.public_summary: Lite boss for role-specialized causal counterplay; it should test coordination before raw damage.
crypt_brute_1.counterplay_hint: Search the focus object and coordinate roles before committing to damage.
crypt_brute_1.defeat_description: Defeat Pocket Idol Guardian after using the lite challenge's coordination lesson.
